frameworks.name: directed exploration
frameworks.one_line: Uncertainty-driven information-seeking sensitive to relative uncertainty

frameworks.name: random exploration
frameworks.one_line: Stochastic exploration modulated by total uncertainty

frameworks.name: undirected exploration
frameworks.one_line: Choice stochasticity insensitive to uncertainty or value

frameworks.name: trait anxiety latent-factor model
frameworks.one_line: Decomposes anxiety into somatic and cognitive factors relating to exploration

core_question: How do latent factors of trait anxiety relate to directed, random, and undirected exploration strategies under volatility-induced uncertainty?
key_assumptions: Exploration is a hybrid of directed, random, and undirected strategies
Relative and total uncertainty are dissociable signals driving choice
Trait anxiety decomposes into somatic and cognitive latent factors
manipulations: Two independent studies aggregating N = 985 participants
Latent-factor decomposition of trait anxiety scales
key_findings: People used a hybrid of directed, random, and undirected exploration
Strategies were respectively sensitive to relative uncertainty, total uncertainty, and value difference
Somatic anxiety inversely correlated with directed and undirected exploration
Somatic anxiety associated with underestimation of relative uncertainty
interpretations: Trait somatic anxiety selectively modulates uncertainty-driven and value-driven exploration
Reduced directed exploration in somatic anxiety reflects underestimation of relative uncertainty
